diff:
diff --git a/django/core/cache/backends/db.py b/django/core/cache/backends/db.py
--- a/django/core/cache/backends/db.py
+++ b/django/core/cache/backends/db.py
@@ -278,8 +278,8 @@ def _cull(self, db, cursor, now, num):
             )
             deleted_count = cursor.rowcount
             remaining_num = num - deleted_count
-            if remaining_num > self._max_entries:
-                cull_num = remaining_num // self._cull_frequency
+            cull_num = remaining_num // self._cull_frequency
+            if cull_num > 0 and remaining_num > self._max_entries:
                 cursor.execute(
                     connection.ops.cache_key_culling_sql() % table, [cull_num]
                 )
diff --git a/tests/cache/tests.py b/tests/cache/tests.py
--- a/tests/cache/tests.py
+++ b/tests/cache/tests.py
@@ -1361,6 +1361,24 @@ def test_no_query_without_check(self):
                 )
                 self.assertEqual(num_count_queries, 0)
 
+    def test_delete_query_skipped_on_high_cull_frequency(self):
+        cull_delete_cache = caches["cull"]
+        old_cull_freq = cull_delete_cache._cull_frequency
+
+        # CULL_FREQUENCY > MAX_ENTRIES forces
+        # (remaining_num // CULL_FREQUENCY) to evaluate to zero (cull_num)
+        cull_delete_cache._cull_frequency = cull_delete_cache._max_entries * 2
+        self.addCleanup(setattr, cull_delete_cache, "_cull_frequency", old_cull_freq)
+
+        self._perform_cull_test("cull", 50, 49)
+
+        with CaptureQueriesContext(connection) as captured_queries:
+            cull_delete_cache.set("force_cull", "value")
+
+        # Only the expiration DELETE query runs; culling is skipped.
+        num_delete_queries = sum("DELETE" in query["sql"] for query in captured_queries)
+        self.assertEqual(num_delete_queries, 1)
+
     def test_delete_cursor_rowcount(self):
         """
         The rowcount attribute should not be checked on a closed cursor.

labeled_issues.description: cull_num could be negative because the row count and num are not fetched atomically; reviewer required an explicit cull_num > 0 guard before running the culling query.

reviewer_comments.author: vishy0
reviewer_comments.body: As per ticket, should this be specifically just greater than `MAX_ENTRIES + 1` value?

reviewer_comments.author: jacobtylerwalls
reviewer_comments.body: Let's do an explicit `cull_num > 0` check, since it could be negative (the count and the num are not fetched at the same time). In fact, I think it's worth adding a test for this case.

reviewer_comments.author: jacobtylerwalls
reviewer_comments.body:
Instead of the finally clause below, please use instead addCleanup, e.g.
```py
self.addCleanup(setattr, cull_delete_cache, "_cull_frequency", old_cull_freq)
```

reviewer_comments.author: jacobtylerwalls
reviewer_comments.body: As you point out below, there is one delete query still, so this might need a more accurate name.

reviewer_comments.author: jacobtylerwalls
reviewer_comments.body: Great. On second look, I don't think a negative max_entries is realistic, so I removed the test.

reviewer_comments.author: vishy0
reviewer_comments.body:
I was wondering, I missed while making commit!
Also, it's same I thought of at which point could it be negative! and I just made the cull frequency negative.
